content: WebSocket JWT auth 2025: pass token during HTTP upgrade handshake — either as query param (?token=JWT in ws:// URL) or as HTTP-only cookie (browser auto-sends on upgrade). Server validates JWT before accepting connection. Never put JWT in Authorization header for WebSocket (browsers don't support custom headers on WS upgrade). For daemon connections: use static pairing token (not short-lived JWT) in query param, validated server-side on connect. Validate Origin header to prevent CSRF. Always use WSS (TLS). For token renewal on long-lived connections: send refresh token as message over existing connection without dropping it. Socket.IO uses io.use() middleware for handshake auth.
